reforms.description: Korea has significantly increased its investment in R&D, rising from 1.2% of GDP in 1984 to 2.7% in 1996, with a target of reaching 3-4% by 1998. Additionally, the government has initiated a five-year plan for scientific and technological innovation aimed at increasing government expenditures in this area from 3% to 5% of the total budget by 2002, focusing on key technologies and supporting new technology-based firms. The HAN project, which absorbs about 13 percent of government spending on technology, includes significant technological advancements and requires substantial private sector funding.
reforms.package_name: Korean R&D Investment and Innovation Strategy
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D investment is expected to enhance productivity and innovation capabilities.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in R&D investment and the establishment of the HAN project have been realized.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a structural change in R&D funding and strategy.
reforms.source_quote: The five-year plan for scientific and technological innovation seeks to increase government expenditures in this area from the current 3 per cent of the total budget to 5 per cent by the year 2002.

reforms.description: Korea opened its capital markets to foreign investors in July 1998, marking a significant shift in its approach to foreign direct investment (FDI) and aiming to attract substantial foreign capital to aid in industrial restructuring and technological advancement.
reforms.package_name: Foreign Investment and Startup Ecosystem Enhancement
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting foreign capital is expected to stimulate innovation and support the growth of startups.
reforms.status_evidence: The opening of capital markets has been executed as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major policy shift in attracting foreign investment.
reforms.source_quote: Korean capital markets were in effect fully opened to foreign investors in July 1998.

reforms.description: The Korean government committed to introduce legislation facilitating securitisation by August 1998, which aims to assist banks in managing their balance sheets and improving profitability through innovative financial products.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating securitisation is expected to enhance financial stability and support innovation in the banking sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The legislation has been passed but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the financial regulatory framework.
reforms.source_quote: The government has promised to introduce needed legislation by August 1998.

reforms.description: Korea is implementing reforms to improve the efficiency of public spending on technology, which includes increasing the research role of universities and avoiding a tendency to 'pick winners' in technology areas.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving efficiency in public spending is expected to enhance the effectiveness of R&D investments.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms are in the process of being implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, these reforms are more about improving existing processes rather than introducing new funding mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: Improve the efficiency of public spending on technology. Increasing the research role of universities and avoiding a tendency to 'pick winners' in the area of technology would help promote effective R&D activities.

reforms.description: The government should enhance the role of universities and encourage interaction between firms, government research institutes, and universities to foster innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration is expected to improve knowledge transfer and innovation outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives are planned but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to improve existing collaboration mechanisms rather than introduce new structures.
reforms.source_quote: Moreover, it should enhance the role of universities and encourage interaction between firms, government research institutes and universities.
